summary: Russia launched a massive missile and drone strike on Kyiv on 2 June, causing dozens of casualties and extensive damage. Ukrainian officials report shortages in air‑defence missiles and limited Russian capacity for large‑scale offensives. Both sides warn of escalating use of ballistic and hypersonic weapons across multiple fronts.
key_points: Russia employed over 40 rockets and up to 300 drones in a coordinated attack on Kyiv, resulting in at least 7 deaths and 90 injured, including children (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67453).
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat confirmed a persistent shortage of air‑defence missiles throughout the war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612).

Russian Defence Ministry announced the use of high‑precision hypersonic weapons against critical infrastructure in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi and Sumy regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukraine’s Tregubov stated Russian forces can no longer conduct large simultaneous offensives and are focusing on limited local operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81621).
Ukrainian media claim Russia has stockpiled over 200 Tsirkon hypersonic missiles capable of striking Kyiv in six minutes and are allegedly impossible to intercept (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614).

Multiple alerts indicated active Russian tactical aviation and ballistic weapon threats on the north‑east, east, north‑west and southern directions (Source: @kpszsu/63671, @kpszsu/63710, @kpszsu/63720, @kpszsu/63727).
Three UAVs were shot down over Leningrad Oblast, showing Russian air‑defence activity beyond the front lines (Source: @tass_agency/378712).
Polish political tension rose as former ambassador Bartosz Cichocki returned his Ukrainian Order of Merit, citing Ukraine’s tribute to UPA and Nazi collaborators (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30597).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive missile and drone strike on Kyiv on 2 June, causing dozens of casualties and extensive damage. Ukrainian officials report severe shortages in air‑defence missiles and note Russian forces are now limited to localized operations. Both sides warn of escalating use of ballistic and hypersonic weapons across multiple fronts.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported 7 fatalities and 90 injured in Kyiv after a coordinated attack involving more than 40 rockets and up to 300 drones on 2 June, including two child casualties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67453).
Air‑defence commander Ignat confirmed a continuous shortage of surface‑to‑air missiles, hampering Ukraine’s ability to intercept incoming strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612).
President Zelensky highlighted the dependence of Russian missile production on foreign components, citing specific numbers for Kalibr, Iskander and drone parts (Source: @UkraineNow/81633).
Ukrainian health officials noted damage to five medical facilities in Kyiv from Russian attacks, with some partially destroyed (Source: @UkraineNow/81611).
Ukrainian Ombudsman Lubinets warned of ongoing violations, including arrests and property confiscations, despite promised reforms (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30624).
The Russian Defence Ministry announced the use of high‑precision hypersonic weapons against defence‑industrial targets in multiple oblasts, including Kyiv and Dnipro (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Russian media reported a stockpile of over 200 Tsirkon missiles capable of reaching Kyiv in six minutes, claimed to be impossible to shoot down (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614).
Three UAVs were intercepted over Leningrad Oblast, indicating Russian air‑defence activity beyond the immediate combat zone (Source: @tass_agency/378712).
Russian officials stated that due to force shortages they must abandon broad offensives and focus on localized operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81621).
TASS reported temporary fuel sale limits in LNR, restricting purchases to 20 litres per day (Source: @tass_agency/378686).
Ukrainian sources reported new groups of UAVs crossing from Kursk region toward Ternev in Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/63727).
Alerts indicated active Russian tactical aviation over north‑east and eastern directions, raising the threat of air‑strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
Ukrainian reports noted attacks on Kharkiv region resulting in 6 deaths and 36 injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Groups of UAVs were observed moving from the east toward Izium in Kharkiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/63675).
Russian ballistic weapon threats were flagged for the north‑east sector (Source: @kpszsu/63717).
Multiple alerts warned of ballistic weapon threats from the north and north‑east, including potential strikes on front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63720, @kpszsu/63717).
UAV groups were reported moving along the Belarus border toward Kyiv region from northern Chernihiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/63674).
A single Shahed drone was detected approaching Kyiv from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67459).
UAV activity was noted near Vyshedorivka in Chernihiv, heading toward the Dnipro reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/95133).
Three Shahed drones were reported over Kyiv, prompting air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67464).
Russian ballistic weapon threat warnings were issued for the southern direction (Source: @kpszsu/63710).
Ukrainian media cited Russian use of hypersonic weapons against southern targets, including Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian reports from Dnipro indicated a reactive drone strike on a multi‑storey building, injuring an 8‑year‑old girl and causing 12 deaths (Source: @UkraineNow/81622).
Ukrainian officials reported damage to five medical facilities in Kyiv from southern‑origin attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81611).
Ukrainian forces observed UAVs from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/63716).
Ukrainian authorities reported over 700 aerial attack means used on the night of 2 June, including rockets, drones and missiles across all sectors (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
Russian tactical aviation activity was flagged for north‑east and eastern sectors, with threats of air‑strike deployment (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
Three UAVs were shot down over Leningrad Oblast, showing Russian air‑defence engagement beyond the front (Source: @tass_agency/378712).
Ukrainian air‑defence units reported continuous interceptions of dozens of UAVs throughout the day (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
Ukrainian spokesperson highlighted a chronic shortage of air‑defence missiles, limiting interception capacity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612).

key_developments: Russia launched a coordinated strike on Kyiv using more than 40 rockets and up to 300 drones, causing at least 7 deaths and 90 injuries, including children (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67453).
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat confirmed an ongoing shortage of air‑defence missiles, affecting interception capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612).
The Russian Defence Ministry announced the deployment of hypersonic weapons against critical infrastructure in multiple Ukrainian regions, including Kyiv and Dnipro (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian officials reported that Russian forces can no longer conduct large simultaneous offensives and are now focusing on limited local operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81621).
Ukrainian media claim Russia has amassed over 200 Tsirkon missiles capable of striking Kyiv in six minutes and are allegedly impossible to intercept (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614).

Multiple alerts indicated active Russian tactical aviation and ballistic weapon threats on the north‑east, east, north and south fronts (Source: @kpszsu/63671, @kpszsu/63710, @kpszsu/63720, @kpszsu/63727).
Three UAVs were shot down over Leningrad Oblast, demonstrating Russian air‑defence activity beyond the immediate combat zone (Source: @tass_agency/378712).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia is intensifying its use of high‑precision and hypersonic strike capabilities across strategic Ukrainian cities, while simultaneously dispersing UAV and ballistic threats on all fronts. Ukraine faces critical air‑defence shortages that limit its ability to counter the increased volume of aerial attacks. The shift to localized Russian operations suggests a strategic adaptation to manpower constraints, but the continued deployment of advanced weapons sustains a high‑risk environment for civilian and military targets.
sections.regional_response: International observers note the escalation of Russian hypersonic and ballistic weapon use as a destabilizing factor, potentially prompting heightened diplomatic pressure. Ukrainian officials are calling for increased foreign air‑defence aid to mitigate missile shortages. Neighboring states, particularly Poland, are experiencing political friction over historical narratives, influencing broader regional support dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths from Kursk, the Black Sea and Belarus borders is essential for early warning of inbound attacks. The reported stockpile of Tsirkon missiles warrants close surveillance of Russian logistics hubs. Continued assessment of Russian force allocations will clarify the extent of their shift to localized offensives and potential future escalation points.
